summary: Jeff Atwood shares his curated list of geek gift guides for the 2006 holiday season, along with a personal recommendation for the iRiver Clix music player. He positions the post as a resource for finding quirky, offbeat tech gifts while acknowledging his own impulse-buying tendencies. The iRiver Clix is praised as a superior alternative to the iPod due to its broader format support, FM radio, and drag-and-drop simplicity. The post is essentially a link roundup with personal commentary, inviting readers to share additional gift guide recommendations.
key_points: Curates multiple 2006 holiday gift guides from sources like Cool Tools, ThinkGeek, Engadget, and CNET
Personally recommends the 4GB iRiver Clix as an iPod alternative with more features
Highlights the Clix's advantages: subscription services, WMA/OGG support, FM radio, no software required
Emphasizes the value of drag-and-drop simplicity over iTunes-style software dependencies
money_quotes: The only reason to grow up is so you can afford to buy yourself all the crap your parents wouldn't buy you when you were a kid.
It does everything the iPod refuses to do: subscription services, wma/ogg support, FM Radio (w/recording!), Flash Lite games, video playback, and voice recording.
Just plug it in, drag and drop your music on it, then play.
key_insight: The best tech gifts are the ones that prioritize user freedom and simplicity over locked-down ecosystems.
